raw.objective: “…to investigate the effects of age and sex on PHT pharmacokinetics using SL isotopes of PHT or fosphenytoin (FOS) administered IV or IM while patients remained on oral maintenance regimens”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, SEX on half-life, maintenance dose, C0 (concentration before administering the new dose), unbound PHT fraction, and albumin Included:
raw.observations: No significant difference was found between elderly (>65y) and regular adults, nor between men and women. Estimation of Km and Vmax was not achieved, instead, regular CL was estimated, which may not adequately describe the non-linearity in PHT PK.
raw.dose: Steady-state IV and IM doses in patients
raw.structure: 1-compartment model first-order absorption (IM administration) or zero-order infusion (IV) and first-order elimination
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.0174 theta2=0.894 theta3=1.44 omega1=0.255 omega2=0.0485 omega3=0.226 sigma1=0.0296

structural_model.description: 1-compartment model first-order absorption (IM administration) or zero-order infusion (IV) and first-order elimination

study.dose: Steady-state IV and IM doses in patients
study.objective: “…to investigate the effects of age and sex on PHT pharmacokinetics using SL isotopes of PHT or fosphenytoin (FOS) administered IV or IM while patients remained on oral maintenance regimens”

tested: C0 (concentration before administering the new dose)
covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, SEX on half-life, maintenance dose, C0 (concentration before administering the new dose), unbound PHT fraction, and albumin Included:

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
